scholarshipName: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) Central Sector Scheme
provider: Department of Higher Education, Govt. of India
description: Financial support for meritorious students from low-income families to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies.
whoCanApply: Students who are above 80th percentile of successful candidates in the relevant stream from the respective Board of Examination in Class XII.
eligibility: Class 12 passed with >= 80% marks. Must be pursuing a regular degree program (non-correspondence).
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹4.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹12,000 per annum for graduation; ₹20,000 per annum for post-graduation.

provider: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Govt. of India
description: High-quality education opportunities for Other Backward Class (OBC), Economically Backward Class (EBC), and De-Notified, Nomadic & Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNT) students.
whoCanApply: OBC, EBC, and DNT students studying in Class 9 or Class 11 in identified schools.
eligibility: Must have passed Class 8 or Class 10 with at least 60% marks. Enrolled in top-class schools approved by the Ministry.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: Up to ₹75,000 p.a. for Class 9/10; Up to ₹1,25,000 p.a. for Class 11/12.

scholarshipName: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
provider: Ministry of Education, Govt. of India
description: To provide financial assistance to meritorious students from underprivileged families to meet day-to-day expenses while pursuing professional and technical graduation degrees.
whoCanApply: Meritorious students pursuing regular graduate, postgraduate, and professional courses.
eligibility: Above 80th percentile in Class 12 exams. Not receiving any other scholarship benefits.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹4.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹12,000 per annum for the first three years of undergraduate level, and ₹20,000 per annum at postgraduate level.

scholarshipName: AICTE Pragati Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students
provider: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
description: An initiative to support girls pursuing technical education. Encourages empowerment of women through engineering and technology careers.
whoCanApply: Girl candidates admitted to the 1st year of Degree/Diploma level technical courses in AICTE-approved institutions.
eligibility: Maximum of two girls per family are eligible. Admitted via centralized state/central counseling.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹8 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹50,000 per annum for every year of study as lump sum towards college fees, books, equipment, and hostel charges.

scholarshipName: AICTE Saksham Scholarship Scheme for Specially Abled Students
provider: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
description: To support and encourage specially-abled students to pursue technical education. Pragmatic step to uplift students with special physical needs.
whoCanApply: Specially-abled students admitted to the 1st year of technical Degree or Diploma courses.
eligibility: Disability level must be 40% or more. Institution must be AICTE-approved.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹8 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹50,000 per annum towards academic fee allowance, books, computer purchasing, and medical expenses.
requiredDocuments: Disability Certificate issued by Competent Authority

provider: Kendriya Sainik Board (KSB), Ministry of Defence
description: To encourage technical and post-graduate professional education for the dependent wards and widows of deceased/ex-service personnel of Armed Forces.
whoCanApply: Wards and widows of Ex-Servicemen (ESM) and Ex-Coast Guard personnel.
eligibility: Must have scored 60% or above in Class 12 or equivalent diploma. Enrolled in recognized professional courses (B.Tech, MBBS, MBA, BCA, MCA).
incomeCriteria: No specific income limit, prioritized based on service ranks and martyred personnel categories.
benefits: ₹3,000 per month for Girls (₹36,000 p.a.); ₹2,500 per month for Boys (₹30,000 p.a.).

provider: Department of Science & Technology (DST), Govt. of India
description: Scholarship for Higher Education (SHE) under the INSPIRE program. Encourages talented students to pursue basic/natural sciences courses.
whoCanApply: Meritorious students who are within the top 1% of successful candidates in state or central boards Class 12 exams.
eligibility: Top 1% in Class 12. Must be pursuing B.Sc., B.S., or Integrated M.Sc. in natural/basic sciences (Physics, Chemistry, Math, Biology, etc.).
incomeCriteria: Based strictly on Board Merit; no separate income slab, but boards verify candidate details.
benefits: ₹80,000 per annum (₹60,000 scholarship + ₹20,000 summer research project mentorship grant).
requiredDocuments: Endorsement Letter signed by College Principal

provider: Department of School Education & Literacy, Govt. of India
description: To prevent drop-outs of bright students at Class 8 and encourage them to continue study up to secondary level.
whoCanApply: Regular students of Class 9 studying in Government, Local Body, and Government-Aided schools.
eligibility: Minimum 55% marks in Class 7 exam. Must qualify the NMMS state-level examination (Mental Ability & Scholastic Tests).
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹3.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: ₹12,000 per annum (₹1,000 per month) from Class 9 to Class 12.

scholarshipName: Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students
provider: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Govt. of India
description: Providing post-matric financial aids to students belonging to Scheduled Castes to enable them to complete post-secondary education.
whoCanApply: SC students studying in post-matric/post-secondary courses (Class 11, Class 12, Degree, Engineering, Medical, MBA).
eligibility: Must belong to SC category. Enrolled in a recognized institution with a good academic track record.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: Full tuition fee reimbursement (as per state boundaries) + monthly maintenance allowances depending on stream course.

scholarshipName: Post Matric Scholarship for ST Students
provider: Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Govt. of India
description: Financial support for students belonging to Scheduled Tribes to minimize tribal dropouts and encourage higher education levels.
whoCanApply: ST students pursuing recognized post-matric/post-secondary courses (Class 11, 12, Engineering, MBA, Degree).
eligibility: Must belong to Scheduled Tribe (ST) category. Admission in a recognized government or private college.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: Full course fees reimbursement + hostel allowances + textbook grants.

provider: Ministry of Minority Affairs, Govt. of India
description: Financial assistance for meritorious students belonging to minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) to pursue higher studies.
whoCanApply: Minority students pursuing higher education (Class 11, 12, UG/PG, Technical/Vocational courses).
eligibility: Must belong to a notified minority community. Scored not less than 50% marks in the previous final examination.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹2 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: Admission and tuition fees reimbursement + maintenance allowance of up to ₹1,200/month for hostellers.
requiredDocuments: Self-Declaration of Minority Community by Candidate

scholarshipName: Top Class Education Scheme for SC Students
provider: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Govt. of India
description: Promotes quality education amongst SC students by providing full financial support for study in notified premier institutes like IITs, IIMs, NITs, and AIIMS.
whoCanApply: SC students secured admission in notified premier educational institutions on merit.
eligibility: Belong to SC category. Enrolled in top-class premium technical, medical, or management colleges.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹8 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: Full tuition fee reimbursement + living expenses (₹3,000/month) + computer allowance (₹45,000 one-time) + book allowance (₹5,000/year).

scholarshipName: MahaDBT Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Scheme
provider: Directorate of Higher Education, Govt. of Maharashtra
description: Fee concession scheme for Maharashtra students belonging to Economically Backward Class (EBC) to complete professional degree studies.
whoCanApply: Candidates residing in Maharashtra pursuing professional/non-professional degree programs.
eligibility: Admitted through CAP rounds. Resident of Maharashtra. Domicile certificate mandatory.
incomeCriteria: Family income less than ₹8 Lakhs per annum.
benefits: 50% tuition and exam fee concession for professional streams; 100% concession for non-professional streams.
